# Slice: ReopenTask ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user clicks [Reopen] on a completed task. The task is set back to incomplete. An automation then places it at the top of the active list (highest priority, position 1) via AddToTaskList. See [AddToTaskList](add-to-task-list.md). ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI["Completed Tasks View
Reopen button"] CMD["ReopenTask"]:::command EVT["TaskReopened"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view AUTO["Automation:
AddToTaskList
(position = 1)"]:::automation UI -->|"task_id"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 EVT --> AUTO classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff classDef automation fill:#8b5cf6,color:#fff ``` ## Command: ReopenTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The completed task to reopen | **Triggered by:** User clicks the [Reopen] button on a task in the completed tasks view. **Rejection conditions:** - Task is not completed (already active). ## Event: TaskReopened | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was reopened | **Downstream automation:** When TaskReopened is recorded, an automation dispatches AddToTaskList with position = 1, placing the reopened task at the top of the active list (highest priority). This differs from newly added tasks which go to the bottom. Rationale: if a user reopens a task, it is likely urgent. See [AddToTaskList](add-to-task-list.md). ## Wireframe ``` Before (Completed Tasks View): +--------------------------------------------------+ | [< Back to Tasks] Completed Tasks | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Send the invoice~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Order new lightbulbs~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ User clicks [Reopen] on "Send the invoice": +--------------------------------------------------+ | [< Back to Tasks] Completed Tasks | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Order new lightbulbs~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ Active Task List now shows (navigating back): +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (2)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | :: Send the invoice [v] [ x ] | | ^--- reopened task at TOP | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task reappears, initially with a temporary position. The automation dispatches AddToTaskList (position = 1) which places it at the top. Completed count decremented. **CompletedTaskList:** Task removed from completed list. ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Reopen a completed task **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" } - TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" } **When** (command): - ReopenTask { task_id: "task-002" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" } **Note:** The reopened task is placed at the top of the active list (position 1) by the AddToTaskList automation, not by this command directly. --- ### Scenario: Reject reopening a task that is already active **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" } **When** (command): - ReopenTask { task_id: "task-001" } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Task is not completed"

# Slice: ChangeTaskDescription ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user clicks on a task's description text to activate inline editing, changes the text, and confirms the edit (by pressing Enter or clicking away). The task's description is updated. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI["Active Task List
inline edit field"] CMD["ChangeTaskDescription"]:::command EVT["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view UI -->|"task_id, new_description"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: ChangeTaskDescription | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to edit | | new_description | text | The updated description (non-empty) | **Triggered by:** User clicks on a task description in the active list, edits the text inline, and confirms by pressing Enter or blurring the field. **Rejection conditions:** - New description is empty or blank. - New description is identical to the current description (no change). ## Event: TaskDescriptionChanged | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task whose description changed | | description | text | The new description | ## Wireframe ``` Before (normal display): :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] User clicks on the description text: :: [Call the dentist office____] [v] [ x ] ^--- inline editing active, cursor in text field User presses Enter or clicks away: :: Call the dentist office [v] [ x ] ^--- updated description displayed ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task description updated (if task is active). **CompletedTaskList:** Task description updated (if task is completed). ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Change a task's description **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist" } **When** (command): - ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" } --- ### Scenario: Reject changing description to the same value (no change) **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist" } **When** (command): - ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist" } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Description is unchanged" --- ### Scenario: Change description after it was previously changed **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist" } - TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" } **When** (command): - ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" } --- ### Scenario: Reject changing description back to a previously changed value that matches current **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist" } - TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" } **When** (command): - ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Description is unchanged" **Note:** Empty/blank description is prevented by the type system (NonEmptyString domain type), not by command rejection. No GWT scenario is needed for that case.

The task list processes the reprioritization, moving the task and 11→adjusting the positions of other affected tasks. 12→ 13→## Diagram 14→ 15→```mermaid 16→flowchart LR 17→ UI["Active Task List
drag handle"] 18→ CMD["ReprioritizeTask"]:::command 19→ EVT["TaskReprioritized"]:::event 20→ VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view 21→ 22→ UI -->|"task_id, new_position"| CMD 23→ CMD --> EVT 24→ EVT --> VIEW 25→ 26→ classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff 27→ classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff 28→ classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff 29→``` 30→ 31→## Command: ReprioritizeTask 32→ 33→| Field | Type | Description | 34→|-------|------|-------------| 35→| task_id | identifier | The task being moved | 36→| new_position | number | The target position in the list | 37→ 38→**Triggered by:** User grabs a drag handle (::) and drops the task at a new 39→position in the active list. 40→ 41→**Rejection conditions:** 42→- Task dropped in the same position (no change). 43→- Task is not in the list (e.g., completed or deleted task). 44→ 45→## Event: TaskReprioritized 46→ 47→| Field | Type | Description | 48→|-------|------|-------------| 49→| task_id | identifier | The task that was moved | 50→| position | number | The new position in the priority-ordered list | 51→ 52→**Note:** This event is emitted by the task list, which has visibility into all 53→task positions. The task list enforces position consistency so that every active 54→task has a unique, contiguous position. 55→ 56→## Wireframe 57→ 58→``` 59→Before: 60→ 61→ :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] 62→ :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] 63→ :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] 64→ :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] 65→ 66→User drags "Fix the leaky faucet" to the top: 67→ 68→ :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] <-- moved here 69→ :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] 70→ :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] 71→ :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] 72→``` 73→ 74→## Read Model Impact 75→ 76→**ActiveTaskList:** Task order updated. The moved task appears at its new 77→position; other tasks shift to accommodate. 78→ 79→## GWT Scenarios 80→ 81→### Scenario: Move a task to a higher priority (lower position number) 82→ 83→**Given** (prior events on the task list): 84→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } 85→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } 86→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 } 87→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-004", position: 4 } 88→ 89→**When** (command): 90→- ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-003", new_position: 1 } 91→ 92→**Then** (events produced): 93→- TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 } 94→ 95→--- 96→ 97→### Scenario: Move a task to a lower priority (higher position number) 98→ 99→**Given** (prior events on the task list): 100→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } 101→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } 102→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 } 103→ 104→**When** (command): 105→- ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-001", new_position: 3 } 106→ 107→**Then** (events produced): 108→- TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 } 109→ 110→--- 111→ 112→### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing to the same position (no change) 113→ 114→**Given** (prior events on the task list): 115→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } 116→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } 117→ 118→**When** (command): 119→- ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-002", new_position: 2 } 120→ 121→**Then** (error -- no events): 122→- Error: "Task is already at this position" 123→ 124→--- 125→ 126→### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing a task that is not in the list 127→ 128→**Given** (prior events on the task list): 129→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } 130→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } 131→ 132→**When** (command): 133→- ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-099", new_position: 1 } 134→ 135→**Then** (error -- no events): 136→- Error: "Task is not in the list" 137→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. # Slice: ReprioritizeTask ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user drags a task from one position in the active list and drops it at a new position. The task list processes the reprioritization, moving the task and adjusting the positions of other affected tasks. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI["Active Task List
drag handle"] CMD["ReprioritizeTask"]:::command EVT["TaskReprioritized"]:::event VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view UI -->|"task_id, new_position"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: ReprioritizeTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task being moved | | new_position | number | The target position in the list | **Triggered by:** User grabs a drag handle (::) and drops the task at a new position in the active list. **Rejection conditions:** - Task dropped in the same position (no change). - Task is not in the list (e.g., completed or deleted task). ## Event: TaskReprioritized | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was moved | | position | number | The new position in the priority-ordered list | **Note:** This event is emitted by the task list, which has visibility into all task positions. The task list enforces position consistency so that every active task has a unique, contiguous position. ## Wireframe ``` Before: :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] User drags "Fix the leaky faucet" to the top: :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] <-- moved here :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task order updated. The moved task appears at its new position; other tasks shift to accommodate. ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Move a task to a higher priority (lower position number) **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-004", position: 4 } **When** (command): - ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-003", new_position: 1 } **Then** (events produced): - TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 } --- ### Scenario: Move a task to a lower priority (higher position number) **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 } **When** (command): - ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-001", new_position: 3 } **Then** (events produced): - TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 } --- ### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing to the same position (no change) **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } **When** (command): - ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-002", new_position: 2 } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Task is already at this position" --- ### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing a task that is not in the list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } **When** (command): - ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-099", new_position: 1 } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Task is not in the list"

# Slice: DeleteTask ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user clicks the delete button on a task (from either the active list or the completed tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If the user confirms, the task is permanently removed from all views. If the deleted task was active, an automation removes it from the ordered task list (see [RemoveFromTaskList](remove-from-task-list.md)). ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI1["Active Task List
delete button"] UI2["Completed Tasks View
delete button"] DIALOG["Confirmation Dialog"] CMD["DeleteTask"]:::command EVT["TaskDeleted"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view AUTO["Automation:
RemoveFromTaskList"]:::automation UI1 -->|"click x"| DIALOG UI2 -->|"click x"| DIALOG DIALOG -->|"confirm, task_id"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 EVT --> AUTO classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff classDef automation fill:#8b5cf6,color:#fff ``` ## Command: DeleteTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to delete | **Triggered by:** User clicks [x] on any task (active or completed), then clicks [Delete] in the confirmation dialog. **Rejection conditions:** - User clicks [Cancel] in the confirmation dialog (command is never issued). ## Event: TaskDeleted | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was deleted | **Business rule:** Deletion is permanent from the UI perspective. The event stream retains the full history, but the UI provides no way to restore a deleted task. **Downstream automation:** When TaskDeleted is recorded for an active task, an automation dispatches RemoveFromTaskList to remove the task from the ordered task list. See [RemoveFromTaskList](remove-from-task-list.md). If the task was already completed (and thus not in the task list), the automation is a no-op. ## Wireframe ``` User clicks [x] on "Fix the leaky faucet": +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | | Delete Task? | | | | | | | | "Fix the leaky faucet" will be | | | | permanently removed. | | | | | | | | [Cancel] [Delete] | | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ If user clicks [Delete], the task disappears from the list. If user clicks [Cancel], nothing changes. ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task removed (if it was active). Remaining tasks may shift positions. **CompletedTaskList:** Task removed (if it was completed). ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Delete an active task **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" } **When** (command): - DeleteTask { task_id: "task-002" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" } --- ### Scenario: Delete a completed task **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" } - TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" } **When** (command): - DeleteTask { task_id: "task-001" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" } **Note:** The confirmation dialog is a UI concern. If the user clicks Cancel, the command is never issued, so there is no rejection scenario at the command level. Once the DeleteTask command is issued (after confirmation), it always succeeds.

# Slice: AddToTaskList ## Pattern **Automation** ## Summary When a task is created (TaskAdded) or reopened (TaskReopened), it needs to be placed into the ordered task list. This automation listens for those events and dispatches an AddToTaskList command to the task list. - **On TaskAdded:** The task is placed at the bottom of the list (lowest priority). The user can reprioritize it afterward. - **On TaskReopened:** The task is placed at the top of the list (position 1, highest priority). Rationale: if a user reopens a task, it is likely urgent. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR EVT1["TaskAdded"]:::event EVT2["TaskReopened"]:::event AUTO["Automation:
Place task in list"]:::automation CMD["AddToTaskList"]:::command RESULT["TaskAddedToList"]:::event VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view EVT1 --> AUTO EVT2 --> AUTO AUTO --> CMD CMD --> RESULT RESULT --> VIEW classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff classDef automation fill:#8b5cf6,color:#fff ``` ## Triggering Events ### TaskAdded When a new task is created, the automation dispatches: - AddToTaskList { task_id: (from event), position: (bottom of list) } The bottom position is determined by the current number of tasks in the list plus one. ### TaskReopened When a completed task is reopened, the automation dispatches: - AddToTaskList { task_id: (from event), position: 1 } Position 1 places the task at the top (highest priority). ## Command: AddToTaskList | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to place in the list | | position | number | The position to place the task at | **Rejection conditions:** - Task is already in the list (duplicate placement). ## Event: TaskAddedToList | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was placed in the list | | position | number | The position it was placed at | ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** The task receives its authoritative position. If placed at position 1 (reopened task), existing tasks shift down. If placed at the bottom (new task), no shifts needed. ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Newly created task is placed at the bottom of an empty list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - (none -- list is empty) **Trigger** (event observed by automation): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - AddToTaskList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } **Then** (events produced): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } --- ### Scenario: Newly created task is placed at the bottom of a populated list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } **Trigger** (event observed by automation): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet" } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - AddToTaskList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 } **Then** (events produced): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 } --- ### Scenario: Reopened task is placed at the top of the list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 2 } **Trigger** (event observed by automation): - TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - AddToTaskList { task_id: "task-002", position: 1 } **Then** (events produced): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 1 } **Note:** Existing tasks shift down. After this event, task-001 is at position 2 and task-003 is at position 3. --- ### Scenario: Reject adding a task that is already in the list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - AddToTaskList { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Task is already in the list"

automation): 108→- TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" } 109→ 110→**When** (command dispatched by automation): 111→- RemoveFromTaskList { task_id: "task-001" } 112→ 113→**Then** (events produced): 114→- no-op) 121→ 122→**Given** (prior events on the task list): 123→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } 124→- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } 125→- TaskRemovedFromList { task_id: "task-002" } 126→ 127→**Trigger** (event observed by automation): 128→- TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" } 129→ 130→**When** (command dispatched by automation): 131→- # Slice: RemoveFromTaskList ## Pattern **Automation** ## Summary When a task is completed (TaskCompleted) or deleted (TaskDeleted), it should be removed from the ordered task list. This automation listens for those events and dispatches a RemoveFromTaskList command to the task list. - **On TaskCompleted:** The completed task is removed from the active ordering. - **On TaskDeleted:** The deleted task is removed from the active ordering (if it was active). If the task was already completed (not in the list), this is a no-op -- the rejection is handled gracefully. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR EVT1["TaskCompleted"]:::event EVT2["TaskDeleted"]:::event AUTO["Automation:
Remove task from list"]:::automation CMD["RemoveFromTaskList"]:::command RESULT["TaskRemovedFromList"]:::event VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view EVT1 --> AUTO EVT2 --> AUTO AUTO --> CMD CMD --> RESULT RESULT --> VIEW classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff classDef automation fill:#8b5cf6,color:#fff ``` ## Triggering Events ### TaskCompleted When a task is marked as done, the automation dispatches: - RemoveFromTaskList { task_id: (from event) } ### TaskDeleted When a task is permanently removed, the automation dispatches: - RemoveFromTaskList { task_id: (from event) } If the deleted task was already completed (and thus not in the task list), the command will be rejected, which the automation handles gracefully (no error surfaced to the user). ## Command: RemoveFromTaskList | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to remove from the list | **Rejection conditions:** - Task is not in the list (already removed or never added). ## Event: TaskRemovedFromList | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was removed from the list | ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** The task is removed from the ordered position list. Remaining tasks shift up to close the position gap, maintaining contiguous positions. ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Completed task is removed from the list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 } **Trigger** (event observed by automation): - TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - RemoveFromTaskList { task_id: "task-002" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskRemovedFromList { task_id: "task-002" } **Note:** After removal, task-001 remains at position 1 and task-003 shifts up to position 2. --- ### Scenario: Deleted active task is removed from the list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } **Trigger** (event observed by automation): - TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - RemoveFromTaskList { task_id: "task-001" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskRemovedFromList { task_id: "task-001" } **Note:** After removal, task-002 shifts up to position 1. --- ### Scenario: Deleted completed task is not in the list (graceful no-op) **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } - TaskRemovedFromList { task_id: "task-002" } **Trigger** (event observed by automation): - TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - RemoveFromTaskList { task_id: "task-002" } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Task is not in the list" **Note:** This rejection is expected and handled gracefully by the automation. The task was already removed when it was completed. No user-visible error occurs.

file.content:
---
title: "Epic: Task Management Workflow"
status: open
priority: 1
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:26:57.912680-08:00"
---

## Workflow: Task Management

### Overview
A single, unified workflow covering the full lifecycle of a task in a
prioritized todo list. The user captures tasks, organizes them by priority
through drag-and-drop reordering, tracks completion, and removes tasks that are
no longer needed.

### User Goal
1. Rapid capture -- quickly add tasks so nothing falls through the cracks
2. Clear prioritized visibility -- always see an ordered list showing what to do next
3. Completion satisfaction -- mark things done and watch the list shrink

### Actors
- User (single person, no authentication)

### Vertical Slices (8)
1. AddTask (Command) + ActiveTaskList (View) -- implemented together
2. CompleteTask (Command)
3. CompletedTaskList (View)
4. ReopenTask (Command)
5. ChangeTaskDescription (Command)
6. ReprioritizeTask (Command)
7. DeleteTask (Command)

### Implementation Order
Start with AddTask + ActiveTaskList together (can't test Add without View).
Then CompleteTask, CompletedTaskList, ReopenTask, ChangeTaskDescription,
ReprioritizeTask, DeleteTask in sequence.

### Architecture
- Commanded aggregate-per-command pattern via commanded_boilerplate
- Single TaskProjector projecting to Ecto schemas
- TodoList.Tasks context module as API boundary
- LiveView with streams + PubSub subscription for real-time updates
- Three test layers: aggregate unit, projector integration, LiveView E2E

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/overview.md

    14→

    16→User types a task description and submits it. A new task is created at the

    28→**Filter:** completed = false AND deleted = false
    29→**Order:** By position ascending (position 1 = highest priority)
    30→
    31→### Acceptance Criteria -- AddTask Command
    32→
    33→#### Scenario: Add task to an empty list
    34→- [ ] **Given**: (no prior events)
    35→- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Buy groceries" }
    36→- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
    37→
    38→#### Scenario: Add task to a list with existing tasks (new task goes to bottom)
    39→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
    40→- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }
    41→- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }
    42→
    43→#### Scenario: Add task when some tasks completed (position based on count)
    44→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, position: 2 }, TaskCompleted { task-001 }
    45→- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Write thank-you notes" }
    46→- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Write thank-you notes", position: 3 }
    47→
    48→### Acceptance Criteria -- ActiveTaskList View
    49→
    50→#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to bottom of empty list
    51→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [], completed_count: 0 }
    52→- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
    53→- [ ] **Then**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task-001, "Buy groceries", pos: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }
    54→

    56→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks

    58→- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks in list, new one at bottom

    60→#### Scenario: TaskCompleted removes task and increments count

    66→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 2
    67→- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
    68→- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks, reopened at position 1, others shift down, completed_count: 1
    69→

    71→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001 "Call the dentist"
    72→- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }
    73→- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Call the dentist office"

    76→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001, completed_count: 1
    77→- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-002" } (completed task)
    78→- [ ] **Then**: No change to active list

    81→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks

    83→- [ ] **Then**: task-003 at position 1, others shift down

    93→- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

    97→- Aggregate-per-command pattern via commanded_boilerplate

---
title: AddTask + ActiveTaskList
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:23.653757-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slices: AddTask (Command) + ActiveTaskList (View)

**Pattern**: Command + View (implemented together -- can't test Add without a View)

### AddTask Command
User types a task description and submits it. A new task is created at the
bottom of the active list with the given description and a default position.

**Command fields:** description (text, non-empty)
**Event:** TaskAdded { task_id, description, position }
**Rejection:** Description is empty or blank (prevented by type system)

### ActiveTaskList View
Read model projecting all events into an ordered list of active (incomplete,
non-deleted) tasks. Powers the main view.

**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description, position }, completed_count
**Filter:** completed = false AND deleted = false
**Order:** By position ascending (position 1 = highest priority)

### Acceptance Criteria -- AddTask Command

#### Scenario: Add task to an empty list
- [ ] **Given**: (no prior events)
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Buy groceries" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }

#### Scenario: Add task to a list with existing tasks (new task goes to bottom)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }

#### Scenario: Add task when some tasks completed (position based on count)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, position: 2 }, TaskCompleted { task-001 }
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Write thank-you notes" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Write thank-you notes", position: 3 }

### Acceptance Criteria -- ActiveTaskList View

#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to bottom of empty list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [], completed_count: 0 }
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task-001, "Buy groceries", pos: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }

#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to bottom of populated list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks in list, new one at bottom

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted removes task and increments count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 2

#### Scenario: TaskReopened adds task to top and decrements count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks, reopened at position 1, others shift down, completed_count: 1

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates active task description
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001 "Call the dentist"
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Call the dentist office"

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for completed task -- no effect on active list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-002" } (completed task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to active list

#### Scenario: TaskReprioritized moves task to new position
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks
- [ ] **When**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: task-003 at position 1, others shift down

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes active task
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for completed task decrements count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 1 task, completed_count: 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-005" } (completed task)
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

### Implementation Notes
- Three test layers: aggregate unit tests, projector integration tests, LiveView E2E tests
- Aggregate-per-command pattern via commanded_boilerplate
- TaskProjector handles projection to Ecto schema
- TodoList.Tasks context as API boundary
- Domain types: TaskDescription, TaskId, TaskPosition (via newt)

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/add-task.md, active-task-list.md

---
title: CompleteTask
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:32.549169-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slice: CompleteTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks the checkbox on an active task to mark it as done. The task
disappears from the active list and appears in the completed tasks view.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskCompleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** Task is already completed

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Complete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: CompleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Reject completing an already-completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: CompleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyCompleted

### Implementation Notes
- Uses aggregate-per-command pattern (separate CompleteTask command module)
- Aggregate needs only: task_id, completed (boolean)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyCompleted (via newt)
- Read model impact tested via ActiveTaskList scenarios (already covered in slice 1)

    10→
    11→## Slice: CompletedTaskList

    13→**Pattern**: View (State View / Read Model)

---
title: CompletedTaskList
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:45.442732-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slice: CompletedTaskList

**Pattern**: View (State View / Read Model)

### Description
Read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
tasks. Powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
and reopen or delete tasks.

**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description }
**Filter:** completed = true AND deleted = false
**Order:** By completion time descending (most recently completed first)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: TaskAdded has no effect on completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds task to the completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds most recent completion to the top
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-002 at top, task-001 below (most recent first)

#### Scenario: TaskReopened removes task from completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: Only task-001 remains

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates completed task description
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001 "Buy groceries"
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries and snacks" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Buy groceries and snacks"

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for non-completed task -- no effect
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes completed task
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Only task-002 remains

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for active task -- no effect on completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list

### Implementation Notes
- Same TaskProjector handles both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList projections
- Projection schema tracks completed status and completion timestamp
- TodoList.Tasks.completed_tasks() query function returns completed tasks ordered by completion_time desc
- Completed tasks LiveView at /tasks/completed

---

title: ReopenTask
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:54.067118-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slice: ReopenTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks [Reopen] on a completed task. The task is set back to incomplete
and placed at the top of the active list (highest priority position).

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskReopened { task_id }
**Business rule:** Reopened task placed at top (highest priority) -- rationale: likely urgent if user reopens it
**Rejection:** Task is not completed (already active)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Reopen a completed task (goes to top of active list)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskCompleted { task-002 }
- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Reject reopening a task that is already active
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskNotCompleted

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, completed (boolean)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskNotCompleted (via newt)
- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList adds to top (position 1, shift others), CompletedTaskList removes task

---

---
title: ChangeTaskDescription
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:28:05.416279-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slice: ChangeTaskDescription

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks on a task's description text to activate inline editing, changes
the text, and confirms (Enter or blur). The task's description is updated.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_description (text, non-empty)
**Event:** TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id, description }
**Rejection:** Empty/blank description (type system) OR identical to current (no change)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Change a task's description
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }

#### Scenario: Reject changing description to the same value (no change)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged

#### Scenario: Change description after it was previously changed
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }

#### Scenario: Reject changing to current value after prior change
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, description (current value)
- Domain error type: TodoList.DescriptionUnchanged (via newt)
- Empty/blank validation handled by TaskDescription newt type, not command rejection
- Read model impact: both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList update description

---

---
title: ReprioritizeTask
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:28:14.983549-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slice: ReprioritizeTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User drags a task from one position in the active list and drops it at a new
position. The task's priority changes relative to the other tasks.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_position (number)
**Event:** TaskReprioritized { task_id, position }
**Rejection:** Task dropped in same position (no change)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Move task to higher priority (lower position number)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAdded { task-003, pos: 3 }, TaskAdded { task-004, pos: 4 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-003", new_position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }

#### Scenario: Move task to lower priority (higher position number)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAdded { task-003, pos: 3 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-001", new_position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 }

#### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing to the same position (no change)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-002", new_position: 2 }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyAtPosition

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, position (current value)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyAtPosition (via newt)
- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList shifts positions of surrounding tasks
- LiveView triggers via drag-and-drop (phx-hook for sortable)

---

---
title: DeleteTask
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:28:23.845977-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slice: DeleteTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks the delete button on a task (from either active list or completed
tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If confirmed, the task is permanently
removed from all views.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskDeleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** None at command level (confirmation dialog is a UI concern)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Delete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Delete a completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }

### Implementation Notes
- Simplest command: no rejection conditions at domain level
- Confirmation dialog is purely a UI/LiveView concern
- Deletion is permanent from UI perspective (events preserved in stream)
- Read model impact: removed from both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList
- If deleted task was completed, ActiveTaskList.completed_count decrements
- Delete available from both active and completed views

---

---
title: "Epic: Task Management Workflow"
status: open
priority: 1
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:26:57.912680-08:00"
---

## Workflow: Task Management

### Overview
A single, unified workflow covering the full lifecycle of a task in a
prioritized todo list. The user captures tasks, organizes them by priority
through drag-and-drop reordering, tracks completion, and removes tasks that are
no longer needed.

### User Goal
1. Rapid capture -- quickly add tasks so nothing falls through the cracks
2. Clear prioritized visibility -- always see an ordered list showing what to do next
3. Completion satisfaction -- mark things done and watch the list shrink

### Actors
- User (single person, no authentication)

### Vertical Slices (8)
1. AddTask (Command) + ActiveTaskList (View) -- implemented together
2. CompleteTask (Command)
3. CompletedTaskList (View)
4. ReopenTask (Command)
5. ChangeTaskDescription (Command)
6. ReprioritizeTask (Command)
7. DeleteTask (Command)

### Implementation Order
Start with AddTask + ActiveTaskList together (can't test Add without View).
Then CompleteTask, CompletedTaskList, ReopenTask, ChangeTaskDescription,
ReprioritizeTask, DeleteTask in sequence.

### Architecture
- Commanded aggregate-per-command pattern via commanded_boilerplate
- Single TaskProjector projecting to Ecto schemas
- TodoList.Tasks context module as API boundary
- LiveView with streams + PubSub subscription for real-time updates
- Three test layers: aggregate unit, projector integration, LiveView E2E

---

input.content:
---
title: AddTask + ActiveTaskList
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:23.653757-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slices: AddTask (Command) + ActiveTaskList (View)

**Pattern**: Command + View (implemented together -- can't test Add without a View)

### AddTask Command
User types a task description and submits it. A new task is created with the
given description. Positioning is handled separately by the AddToTaskList
automation (see AddToTaskList slice).

**Command fields:** description (text, non-empty)
**Event:** TaskAdded { task_id, description }
**Rejection:** Description is empty or blank (prevented by type system)

**Note:** TaskAdded no longer includes a position field. Positioning is owned
by the TaskList aggregate via the AddToTaskList automation.

### ActiveTaskList View
Read model projecting all events into an ordered list of active (incomplete,
non-deleted) tasks. Powers the main view.

**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description, position }, completed_count
**Filter:** completed = false AND deleted = false
**Order:** By position ascending (position 1 = highest priority)

**Events handled:** TaskAdded, TaskAddedToList, TaskCompleted, TaskReopened,
TaskDescriptionChanged, TaskReprioritized, TaskRemovedFromList, TaskDeleted

### Acceptance Criteria -- AddTask Command

#### Scenario: Add task to an empty list
- [ ] **Given**: (no prior events)
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Buy groceries" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Buy groceries" }

#### Scenario: Add task to a list with existing tasks
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }

#### Scenario: Add task when some tasks completed
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001 }, TaskAdded { task-002 }, TaskCompleted { task-001 }
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Write thank-you notes" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Write thank-you notes" }

### Acceptance Criteria -- ActiveTaskList View

#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task with temporary position
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [], completed_count: 0 }
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }
- [ ] **Then**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task-001, "Buy groceries", pos: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }
- Note: Position 1 is temporary (max + 1). Authoritative position comes from TaskAddedToList.

#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task with temporary position to populated list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks at positions 1, 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }
- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks in list, new one at temporary position 3 (max + 1)

#### Scenario: TaskAddedToList sets authoritative position
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks (positions 1, 2, 3)
- [ ] **When**: TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: Position confirmed, no change needed (temporary matched authoritative)

#### Scenario: TaskAddedToList places reopened task at top, shifting others
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-004", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: task-004 at position 1, existing tasks shift down (positions +1)

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted removes task and increments count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 2

#### Scenario: TaskRemovedFromList removes task from ordered positions
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks at positions 1, 2, 3
- [ ] **When**: TaskRemovedFromList { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 2 tasks remain, positions shift up to close gap (1, 2)

#### Scenario: TaskReopened marks task active, decrements completed count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks, reopened with temporary position (max + 1), completed_count: 1

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates active task description
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001 "Call the dentist"
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Call the dentist office"

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for completed task -- no effect on active list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-002" } (completed task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to active list

#### Scenario: TaskReprioritized moves task to new position
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks
- [ ] **When**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: task-003 at position 1, others shift down

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes active task
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for completed task decrements count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 1 task, completed_count: 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-005" } (completed task)
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

### Implementation Notes
- Three test layers: aggregate unit tests, projector integration tests, LiveView E2E tests
- Aggregate-per-command pattern via commanded_boilerplate
- TaskProjector handles projection to Ecto schema
- TodoList.Tasks context as API boundary
- Domain types: TaskDescription, TaskId (via newt)
- Projector handles TaskAddedToList and TaskRemovedFromList from TaskList stream

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/add-task.md, active-task-list.md

Read model projecting all events into an ordered list of active (incomplete,
non-deleted) tasks. Powers the main view.

**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description, position }, completed_count
**Filter:** completed = false AND deleted = false
**Order:** By position ascending (position 1 = highest priority)

**Events handled:** TaskAdded, TaskAddedToList, TaskCompleted, TaskReopened,
TaskDescriptionChanged, TaskReprioritized, TaskRemovedFromList, TaskDeleted

### Acceptance Criteria -- AddTask Command

#### Scenario: Add task to an empty list
- [ ] **Given**: (no prior events)
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Buy groceries" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Buy groceries" }

#### Scenario: Add task to a list with existing tasks
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }

#### Scenario: Add task when some tasks completed
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001 }, TaskAdded { task-002 }, TaskCompleted { task-001 }
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Write thank-you notes" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Write thank-you notes" }

### Acceptance Criteria -- ActiveTaskList View

#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task with temporary position
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [], completed_count: 0 }
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }
- [ ] **Then**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task-001, "Buy groceries", pos: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }
- Note: Position 1 is temporary (max + 1). Authoritative position comes from TaskAddedToList.

#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task with temporary position to populated list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks at positions 1, 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }
- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks in list, new one at temporary position 3 (max + 1)

#### Scenario: TaskAddedToList sets authoritative position
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks (positions 1, 2, 3)
- [ ] **When**: TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: Position confirmed, no change needed (temporary matched authoritative)

#### Scenario: TaskAddedToList places reopened task at top, shifting others
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-004", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: task-004 at position 1, existing tasks shift down (positions +1)

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted removes task and increments count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 2

#### Scenario: TaskRemovedFromList removes task from ordered positions
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks at positions 1, 2, 3
- [ ] **When**: TaskRemovedFromList { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 2 tasks remain, positions shift up to close gap (1, 2)

#### Scenario: TaskReopened marks task active, decrements completed count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks, reopened with temporary position (max + 1), completed_count: 1

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates active task description
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001 "Call the dentist"
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Call the dentist office"

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for completed task -- no effect on active list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-002" } (completed task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to active list

#### Scenario: TaskReprioritized moves task to new position
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks
- [ ] **When**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: task-003 at position 1, others shift down

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes active task
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for completed task decrements count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 1 task, completed_count: 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-005" } (completed task)
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

### Implementation Notes
- Three test layers: aggregate unit tests, projector integration tests, LiveView E2E tests
- Aggregate-per-command pattern via commanded_boilerplate
- TaskProjector handles projection to Ecto schema
- TodoList.Tasks context as API boundary
- Domain types: TaskDescription, TaskId (via newt)
- Projector handles TaskAddedToList and TaskRemovedFromList from TaskList stream

title: CompleteTask
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task

blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slice: CompleteTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks the checkbox on an active task to mark it as done. The task
disappears from the active list and appears in the completed tasks view.
An automation then removes the task from the ordered task list (see
RemoveFromTaskList slice).

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskCompleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** Task is already completed
**Downstream automation:** TaskCompleted triggers RemoveFromTaskList dispatch

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Complete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **When**: CompleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Reject completing an already-completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: CompleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyCompleted

### Implementation Notes
- Uses aggregate-per-command pattern (separate CompleteTask command module)
- Aggregate needs only: task_id, completed (boolean)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyCompleted (via newt)
- Event handler subscribes to TaskCompleted and dispatches RemoveFromTaskList
- Read model impact tested via ActiveTaskList scenarios (already covered in slice 1)

title: CompletedTaskList
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:45.442732-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slice: CompletedTaskList

**Pattern**: View (State View / Read Model)

### Description
Read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
tasks. Powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
and reopen or delete tasks.

**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description }
**Filter:** completed = true AND deleted = false
**Order:** By completion time descending (most recently completed first)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: TaskAdded has no effect on completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }
- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds task to the completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds most recent completion to the top
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-002 at top, task-001 below (most recent first)

#### Scenario: TaskReopened removes task from completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: Only task-001 remains

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates completed task description
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001 "Buy groceries"
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries and snacks" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Buy groceries and snacks"

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for non-completed task -- no effect
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes completed task
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Only task-002 remains

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for active task -- no effect on completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list

### Implementation Notes
- Same TaskProjector handles both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList projections
- Projection schema tracks completed status and completion timestamp
- TodoList.Tasks.completed_tasks() query function returns completed tasks ordered by completion_time desc
- Completed tasks LiveView at /tasks/completed

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for non-completed task -- no effect

---
title: ReopenTask
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:54.067118-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slice: ReopenTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks [Reopen] on a completed task. The task is set back to incomplete.
An automation then places it at the top of the active list (highest priority,
position 1) via AddToTaskList. See AddToTaskList slice.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskReopened { task_id }
**Business rule:** Reopened task placed at top (highest priority) -- rationale: likely urgent if user reopens it
**Rejection:** Task is not completed (already active)
**Downstream automation:** TaskReopened triggers AddToTaskList { position: 1 } dispatch

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Reopen a completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- Note: Repositioning at top (position 1) is handled by AddToTaskList automation, not this command.

#### Scenario: Reject reopening a task that is already active
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }
- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskNotCompleted

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, completed (boolean)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskNotCompleted (via newt)
- Event handler subscribes to TaskReopened and dispatches AddToTaskList { position: 1 }
- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList adds with temp position, CompletedTaskList removes task

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/reopen-task.md

---
title: ChangeTaskDescription
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:28:05.416279-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slice: ChangeTaskDescription

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks on a task's description text to activate inline editing, changes
the text, and confirms (Enter or blur). The task's description is updated.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_description (text, non-empty)
**Event:** TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id, description }
**Rejection:** Empty/blank description (type system) OR identical to current (no change)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Change a task's description
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }

#### Scenario: Reject changing description to the same value (no change)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged

#### Scenario: Change description after it was previously changed
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }

#### Scenario: Reject changing to current value after prior change
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, description (current value)
- Domain error type: TodoList.DescriptionUnchanged (via newt)
- Empty/blank validation handled by TaskDescription newt type, not command rejection
- Read model impact: both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList update description

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/change-task-description.md

---
title: ReprioritizeTask
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:28:14.983549-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slice: ReprioritizeTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User drags a task from one position in the active list and drops it at a new
position. The **TaskList aggregate** processes the reprioritization, moving the
task and adjusting positions of other affected tasks.

**Aggregate:** TaskList (fixed stream identifier, NOT per-task)
**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_position (number)
**Event:** TaskReprioritized { task_id, position }
**Rejection:** Task dropped in same position (no change) OR task not in list

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Move task to higher priority (lower position number)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAddedToList { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAddedToList { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAddedToList { task-003, pos: 3 }, TaskAddedToList { task-004, pos: 4 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-003", new_position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }

#### Scenario: Move task to lower priority (higher position number)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAddedToList { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAddedToList { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAddedToList { task-003, pos: 3 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-001", new_position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 }

#### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing to the same position (no change)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAddedToList { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAddedToList { task-002, pos: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-002", new_position: 2 }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyAtPosition

#### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing a task not in the list
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAddedToList { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAddedToList { task-002, pos: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-099", new_position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskNotInList

### Implementation Notes
- Routes to TaskList aggregate (fixed stream), NOT per-task aggregate
- TaskList aggregate tracks ordered positions of ALL active tasks
- Domain error types: TodoList.TaskAlreadyAtPosition, TodoList.TaskNotInList (via newt)
- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList shifts positions of surrounding tasks
- LiveView triggers via drag-and-drop (phx-hook for sortable)

---

---
title: DeleteTask
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:28:23.845977-08:00"
blocks:
  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
---

## Slice: DeleteTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks the delete button on a task (from either active list or completed
tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If confirmed, the task is permanently
removed from all views. If the deleted task was active, an automation removes it
from the ordered task list (see RemoveFromTaskList slice).

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskDeleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** None at command level (confirmation dialog is a UI concern)
**Downstream automation:** TaskDeleted triggers RemoveFromTaskList dispatch (if task was active)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Delete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Delete a completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }

### Implementation Notes
- Simplest command: no rejection conditions at domain level
- Confirmation dialog is purely a UI/LiveView concern
- Deletion is permanent from UI perspective (events preserved in stream)
- Event handler subscribes to TaskDeleted and dispatches RemoveFromTaskList
- If deleted task was already completed (not in list), RemoveFromTaskList is rejected gracefully
- Read model impact: removed from both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList
- If deleted task was completed, ActiveTaskList.completed_count decrements
- Delete available from both active and completed views

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/delete-task.md

**Rejection:** None at command level (confirmation dialog is a UI concern)
**Downstream automation:** TaskDeleted triggers RemoveFromTaskList dispatch (if task was active)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Delete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Delete a completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }

### Implementation Notes
- Simplest command: no rejection conditions at domain level
- Confirmation dialog is purely a UI/LiveView concern
- Deletion is permanent from UI perspective (events preserved in stream)
- Event handler subscribes to TaskDeleted and dispatches RemoveFromTaskList
- If deleted task was already completed (not in list), RemoveFromTaskList is rejected gracefully
- Read model impact: removed from both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList
- If deleted task was completed, ActiveTaskList.completed_count decrements
- Delete available from both active and completed views

### Description
User clicks the delete button on a task (from either active list or completed
tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If confirmed, the task is permanently
removed from all views.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskDeleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** None at command level (confirmation dialog is a UI concern)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Delete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Delete a completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }

title: AddToTaskList
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-07T14:58:44.941348-08:00"
---

## Slice: AddToTaskList

**Pattern**: Automation

### Description
When a task is created (TaskAdded) or reopened (TaskReopened), it needs to be
placed into the ordered task list. An event handler listens for those events and
dispatches an AddToTaskList command to the TaskList aggregate.

- **On TaskAdded:** Dispatches AddToTaskList with position = bottom (current count + 1).
- **On TaskReopened:** Dispatches AddToTaskList with position = 1 (top, highest priority).

**Aggregate:** TaskList (fixed stream identifier)
**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), position (number)
**Event:** TaskAddedToList { task_id, position }
**Rejection:** Task is already in the list (duplicate placement)

### Acceptance Criteria -- AddToTaskList Command (TaskList Aggregate)

#### Scenario: Place newly created task at bottom of empty list
- [ ] **Given**: (no prior events on the task list)
- [ ] **Trigger**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }
- [ ] **When**: AddToTaskList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 }

#### Scenario: Place newly created task at bottom of populated list
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAddedToList { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAddedToList { task-002, pos: 2 }
- [ ] **Trigger**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }
- [ ] **When**: AddToTaskList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 }

#### Scenario: Place reopened task at top of list
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAddedToList { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAddedToList { task-003, pos: 2 }
- [ ] **Trigger**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **When**: AddToTaskList { task_id: "task-002", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 1 }
- Note: Existing tasks shift down (task-001 -> pos 2, task-003 -> pos 3)

#### Scenario: Reject adding a task already in the list
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAddedToList { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAddedToList { task-002, pos: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: AddToTaskList { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyInList

### Implementation Notes
- Introduces the TaskList aggregate (fixed stream with constant list_id)
- Event handler uses Commanded.Event.Handler to subscribe to TaskAdded and TaskReopened
- Handler determines bottom position by querying current list size (or from aggregate state)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyInList (via newt)
- No infinite loops: TaskAddedToList does NOT trigger further automations

---
